mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in monthly employment payrolls signals impending recession risk, triggering downward revisions to corporate earnings forecasts and depressing S&P 500 equity valuations within a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in housing starts and consumer credit growth indicates a synchronized pullback in durable goods spending, which reduces aggregate demand and forces downward earnings revisions for consumer-facing equities.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 900 and RSAFS declines > 5% month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in at least 55% of historical occurrences, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression shows a p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during an expansion phase reflects peak capacity utilization and pricing power, driving short-term equity momentum as corporate margins expand ahead of the business cycle peak.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with rising capacity utilization
failure_condition: INDPRO increases correlate with negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations, or the rolling Spearman correlation drops below -0.05 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth consistently exceeding 2.5% annualized sustains broad-based corporate profit expansion, lifting equity valuations across sectors and generating positive medium-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth above 2.5% fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances, or the forward return median falls below zero over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating headline inflation forces market participants to price in aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which raises the risk-free rate and compresses equity risk premiums, leading to negative short-term equity performance.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above the threshold do not precede negative 20-day forward returns in at least 55% of cases, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.2 over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, thereby driving positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of triggered periods, or the interaction coefficient in a panel regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle slowdown, decelerating real GDP growth triggers systematic downward revisions to consensus earnings estimates, which mechanically depresses equity valuations and generates negative medium-term forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate falls below 0.5%
failure_condition: GDP deceleration below the threshold fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of historical episodes, or the forward return distribution shows no significant skew toward negative values.
